quote_of_day.text: Having a sacred animal is the most retarded thing a culture could do. Eating beef is based, it's just a fucking animal. If you think it's a god get your malnutritioned brain checked. >muh milk Every other mammal gives milk, why is cow special.
quote_of_day.name: Savarkar-pilled

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of speculation swirled around Bharatchan's departure, drawing a storm of doxxing concerns and ownership questions. Anon probed the void left by Bharatchan's exit.

A front of state-based opinionatedness moved in, with one anon asserting that opinions from non-blue states on India were irrelevant. This sparked a scattered drizzle of responses, but the claim did not gain much traction.

Meanwhile, a brief but intense heat wave of pro-colonization sentiment flared up, as an anon openly called for British colonization of India and disparaged 'Pajeets'.

Elsewhere, a light fog of anticipation settled over speculation about an upcoming girl group and debates over sacred animals and dietary prohibitions.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Bharatchan's Departure, Doxxing
articles.body:
Anon questioned what happened to Bharatchan, why he was referred to as "Gayush Dalit," and who the owner of the board was. This was in response to a period of absence.

Anon responded that Bharatchan had stopped running the board due to financial issues, hosting problems, and harassment. The previous owner had trouble with reports, raids from other communities, and internal conflicts.

Another anon mentioned that Bharatchan had been planning to leave for months, and that caste conflicts had been managed by creating a dedicated thread. However, reports and raids from other platforms continued to cause problems.

Anon also discussed the doxxing of a user named Motivanon, who had been doxxed after referencing a workplace conversation and his last name.

Some anons expressed regret over the doxxing, while others noted that it had been easy to do. Anon identified a user named "Swissfag Shiller Vegcuckery" as a Muslim trying to incite civil war in India.

This user was reportedly not an Indian Muslim. The thread also included various discussions on rape, with one anon stating that "penis can't get raped" and that only vaginas can be raped.

This led to a series of jokes and responses. Anons also discussed their interactions with Bharatchan and the challenges of running an imageboard.

One anon noted that running an Indian chan was "literal hell" due to constant reports, raids, and internal conflicts.

articles.title: Anon Claims Opinions from Non-Blue States on India Don't Matter
articles.body:
Anon sparked a discussion by stating that unless someone is from a blue state, their opinions about India and its neighbors are irrelevant.

One respondent countered that every state has its unique strengths and challenges, questioning why some states like Bihar haven't developed an effective economic model despite having fertile plains, rivers, and minerals.

Another anon argued that Bihar's underperformance can't be solely attributed to the Freight Equalization Policy, citing other factors at play. The conversation turned to the topic of state performance and economic indicators.

Anon pointed out that GDP per capita is inversely proportional to population, suggesting that if Uttar Pradesh were split into two, its per capita GDP would increase.

However, another participant argued that GDP per capita is worthless, and overall GDP is a more significant factor. The discussion also touched on regional comparisons, with one anon implying that southern states have an unfair advantage due to historical policies and geography.

A respondent from a Pahadi background noted that smaller states with smaller populations can focus on niche businesses, while another anon mentioned that freight equalization has contributed to the South's slightly better numbers.

Throughout the conversation, various anons exchanged views on state performance, economic indicators, and regional disparities, with some using provocative language to make their points.

articles.title: Anon Calls for British Colonization of India, Slams 'Pajeets'
articles.body:
Anon sparked controversy with a post calling for the British to colonize India again, labeling Indians 'low IQ insects' and 'shit lovers.' The poster expressed a desire for a 'pure European and Western meritocracy' in India.

Anon criticized a group referred to as 'Pajeets,' who they claim are advocating for a 50% women's reservation in parliament and 'dalit propaganda.' One respondent agreed with the OP, characterizing the movement as 'retarded' and suggesting that the group's ideals are misguided.

Another poster, Übermensch, discussed online security, warning that services like ProtonMail may not be as secure as claimed. Übermensch also mentioned exploring alternative methods for maintaining privacy online.

Some respondents expressed a desire to counter the 'Pajeet' movement, with one suggesting the creation of a counter-movement. Others proposed practical steps, such as learning cybersecurity and OSINT skills, to challenge the group.

The discussion also touched on the topic of deanonymization, with one poster cautioning against using certain online services that may compromise user anonymity. Anon also brought up the idea of tracking down and harassing individuals promoting liberal ideologies.

The thread was marked by a tone of hostility towards the 'Pajeets' and a general skepticism towards their ideals and methods.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Upcoming Girl Group
articles.body:
Anon initiated a discussion about an upcoming girl group called 'gobarjeeta kumari'. The poster asked if anyone's sister was auditioning and expressed interest in seeing the group's dynamics, comparing them to other international branches.

One respondent expressed support for the group, mentioning 'NEpyari supremacy' and appreciation for the group's diverse appearance. Another user joked about the group's potential success and the appeal of their purity to their audience.

The discussion took a turn as users began to fantasize about the group's members and potential relationships with fans. Some users made explicit comments about the group's potential dynamics and the appeal of their music.

The conversation remained focused on speculation about the group's composition and dynamics, with users sharing their thoughts on the group's potential success and appeal.

articles.title: Anon debates sacred animals and dietary prohibitions
articles.body:
Anon questioned the concept of sacred animals, calling it "the most retarded thing a culture could do." Anon argued that dietary prohibitions, such as not eating beef or pork, have no logical basis.

Another anon noted that the issue with naming an animal as sacred is that it allows outsiders to mock those who hold such beliefs.

Anon also discussed possible historical reasons behind certain practices, such as circumcision, and why some foods are considered unclean. One respondent called such practices "religious retardation."
